name: Alex Krizhevsky
key_contributions: Created AlexNet (2012) — the convolutional neural network that won ImageNet and launched the deep learning era
Demonstrated that deep CNNs trained on GPUs could dramatically outperform traditional computer vision approaches
Student of Geoffrey Hinton at the University of Toronto
AlexNet's ImageNet victory is widely considered the single event that ignited the deep learning revolution
summary: Alex Krizhevsky created AlexNet, the deep convolutional neural network that won the 2012 ImageNet Large Scale Visual Recognition Challenge by a historic margin, reducing the top-5 error rate from 26% to 15.3%. This single result convinced the machine learning community that deep neural networks trained on GPUs were the future. As a student of Geoffrey Hinton at the University of Toronto, Krizhevsky demonstrated that the ideas Hinton had championed through two AI winters could finally deliver transformative results at scale.
